step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("effectiveness of early second-look tympanoplasty in preventing conversion to modified radical mastoidectomy")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First write up to two sentences analyzing the retrieved evidence, then state your agreement/disagreement with the prior conclusion. Finish with your chosen option in boxed notation.
Based on the retrieved evidence, early second-look tympanoplasty is not supported as a strategy to reduce conversion to modified radical mastoidectomy since such conversions stem primarily from active or persistent cholesteatoma rather than predictable failure of initial treatment. There is no direct evidence suggesting that preemptively scheduling second-look procedures improves outcomes or prevents progression of disease requiring more extensive surgery. I agree with the original conclusion that early second-look tympanoplasty does not effectively reduce conversion rates.
